text.title: error-handling Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for error-handling. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every 4xx and 5xx response must carry: type (URI identifying the error class), title (short human label, stable per type), status (integer matching HTTP status), detail (per-request human description), instance (request path), traceId (correlation ID from the tracing SDK).
rule.rationale: Consistent fields let clients, gateways, and monitoring tools parse errors without ad-hoc string matching. traceId is the critical field for correlating client-reported errors with server traces.

rule.statement: Define ProblemDetail and FieldError as reusable OpenAPI components. Reference them in every 4xx and 5xx response of every operation. Use application/problem+json as the Content-Type, not application/json.
rule.rationale: A $ref keeps the schema in one place. Using application/problem+json lets proxies, gateways, and clients distinguish error responses from normal ones by Content-Type alone.

rule.statement: Define error codes as stable machine-readable slugs (snake_case, versioned per type URI). Codes must be stable across releases — renames break clients silently.
rule.rationale: Clients branch on codes, not on HTTP status alone (e.g., conflict vs duplicate-key vs optimistic-lock all return 409 but need distinct codes). Stable slugs let clients ship independent of server release cycles.

rule.statement: Register three exception handlers: one for ValidationError, one for HTTPException, one for bare Exception (catch-all). Never omit the catch-all — unhandled exceptions must also return ProblemDetail with traceId so support can find the trace.
rule.rationale: Missing the catch-all breaks the error contract: the client receives a 500 with an HTML or empty body instead of ProblemDetail, and the traceId is lost.

rule.statement: Set traceId from the active tracing SDK context (OpenTelemetry trace_id, Datadog dd.trace_id), not from uuid.uuid4(). The traceId in the body must match the trace ID in server logs.
rule.rationale: A freshly generated UUID in the response body never matches any log entry. The OTel trace_id does — it links the client error report directly to the server span.

rule.statement: Never put exception messages, stack traces, SQL queries, connection strings, file paths, or internal hostnames in the detail or errors[] fields. Scrub all user-supplied data of newlines and control characters to prevent log injection.
rule.rationale: Error detail is part of the API response — it reaches browsers, mobile apps, and third-party integrations. DSN strings, table names, and stack frames are confidential and create attack surface.

rule.statement: Reference ProblemDetail in BOTH 4xx AND 5xx responses for every OpenAPI operation. Run spectral or check_problem_refs.py in CI to enforce this.
rule.rationale: Agents add ProblemDetail to 4xx but forget 5xx. 500 responses without a ProblemDetail body break client SDK code-gen that expects a uniform error shape.

rule.statement: Pin type URIs to a versioned path (/errors/v1/validation-error). Never rename or delete a type URI after it ships — that breaks clients who branch on the string.
rule.rationale: RFC 7807 type is a stable identifier, not a display string. Clients may hardcode it in switch/match statements. Version the path so renames introduce a new URI rather than breaking the old one.
